title: Surface theme parity
summary: Keep code blocks, viewers, inspectors, and artifact panels inside the surrounding light/dark theme model so they stay coherent, readable, and accessible.
decision: Whether an embedded code, viewer, or artifact surface follows the active light/dark theme model of the surrounding interface while remaining visually distinct.

intent.purpose: Prevent generated interfaces from dropping dark-only code blocks, terminal chrome, or mismatched artifact viewers into a surface that otherwise follows a different theme model.
intent.failure_mode: Generated UI uses a separate theme model for a code or artifact surface, so a light interface suddenly contains a dark terminal block or a dark interface suddenly contains a bright artifact card that feels detached, less readable, and less accessible.
intent.good_judgment: Keep artifact surfaces theme-matched with the active interface mode while using borders, spacing, typography, and local syntax contrast to signal that the region is code or machine-facing content.
acceptable_variation: make code or artifact surfaces slightly more muted or more bordered than surrounding panels while keeping the same light or dark theme model
use syntax-highlight colors that are retuned for the active surface instead of reusing a dark-terminal palette everywhere
follow approved design-system treatment for inspectors, viewers, or code samples when those surfaces still track the active theme model
preserve strong local distinction through layout, labeling, or borders instead of forcing a separate theme
non_negotiables: no dark-only code block or terminal treatment inside an otherwise light-themed surface unless the brief explicitly requires a separate terminal artifact
no bright light artifact panel inside an otherwise dark-themed surface unless the brief explicitly requires a separate theme
no syntax palette that depends on a mismatched background to stay legible
no artifact surface that implies a different product theme model from the rest of the interface without explicit approval

detection.decision_question: Do code blocks, viewers, and artifact panels follow the active light/dark theme model of the surrounding interface, or do they introduce a separate theme treatment that breaks coherence and readability?
signals: a light interface contains a dark terminal-style code block without an explicit reason
a dark interface contains a light artifact surface that reads like a different theme
syntax colors only remain legible because the surface uses a mismatched background
artifact surfaces feel detached because theme contrast, not local structure, is doing all the separation
embedded viewers imply a different theme model than the surrounding shell

thresholds.pass: artifact surfaces remain clearly identifiable while staying inside the active light or dark theme model
thresholds.warn: a code or artifact surface uses an off-theme treatment that can be corrected by retuning the local surface and syntax colors
thresholds.fail: theme mismatch materially harms readability, accessibility, or visual coherence

low.description: Retheme the local artifact surface to match the surrounding light or dark mode while preserving distinction.

medium.description: Retheme the artifact surface and route the result through design review for accessibility and coherence.

high.description: Block the output and require design review because the mismatched theme treatment undermines readability or accessibility.
